items.title: How fandom became culture’s power center — and a blueprint for Gen Z’s economic influence
items.description: Fandoms sit at the intersection of identity, influence, and spending. Three dynamics explain their accelerating economic power.

items.title: ‘I have a chip on my shoulder.’ Phoebe Gates wants her $185 million AI startup Phia to succeed with ‘no ties to my privilege or my last name’
items.description: The daughter of Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates acknowledges her privilege, but says she’s building her own thing without their help.

items.title: The CEO of a $1 billion AI unicorn says his peers in Silicon Valley want you to fear for your job, but they’re actually first on the chopping block
items.description: Tanmai Gopal of PromptQL said tech people have a bad habit of thinking "this affects me. So it's going to affect everyone like that."

items.title: Peter Thiel and other tech billionaires are publicly shielding their children from the products that made them rich
items.description: As governments race to restrict minors’ social media use, the tech billionaires who built the platforms are imposing strict screen limits at home.

items.title: If the recent AI and crypto shocks upset you, you’re tracking the wrong cycle
items.description: Claude and DeepSeek, separated at birth?
